questions.question: In the following questions, one part of the sentence may or may not have an error. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If the sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'. While people disagree with us or turn us down, we routinely and typically incorrectly interpret it as evidence of enmity.
options: with us or turn us down, we
routinely and typically incorrectly interpret it as evidence of enmity
questions.explanation: The error is in the part mentioned in option C. The sentence is in a cause-effect structure. The first clause is the 'cause' while the second clause is it effect. 'While' is used to denote events or activities happening at the same time or to depict contrast; hence, is incorrect here. 'When' is used to show cause - effect relationship and it is also used in conditional sentences; hence, 'when' should replace 'while' to make the sentence correct. Option C is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, one part of the sentence may or may not have an error. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If the sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'. You defined the contours of your character and the shape of your life by what you say "no" to.
options: and the shape of your life by what you say "no" to
questions.explanation: The error is in the part given in option A. The sentence is in the form a simple statement or a simple fact. And this kind of sentences are always in the form of a simple present tense. 'Define' should be used to make the sentence correct. Option A is the correct answer.

questions.question: Which word/phrase should come at (A)?
questions.explanation: An adjective is needed in the blank to modify the noun 'reforms'. The sentence talks about Saudi Arabia announcing some kind of reforms that will decide what women can and can't do on their own. 'Sweeping(adjective)' means 'wide in range or effect; far-reaching' and it fits in the blank grammatically and makes the sentence meaningful. 'Nervous(adjective)' is contextually incorrect; it does not lend the sentence a proper meaningful as 'sweeping' does. The other two options 'movement(noun)' and 'shock(noun/verb)' are grammatically incorrect. Option B is the correct answer.

questions.question: Which word/phrase should come at (B)?
questions.explanation: A verb is needed in the blank to show the action performed by the subject 'the publication of the new laws'. And as the passage refers to an event that has already occurred, a past form of verb will fit in the blank. 'Prompted' means 'caused, produced or elicited (an action or feeling)' and it fits in the blank and makes the sentence meaningful. 'Developed' is also a past form of verb but it is contextually inappropriate, and D is a better choice. 'Recoil' and 'start', both as verbs are in their base forms and both are also nouns; they are grammatically incorrect in the blank. Option D is the correct answer.

questions.question: Which word/phrase should come at (C)?
questions.explanation: The verb 'requiring' indicates the action of 'need' of something (a noun). Hence, 'permission'(consent or authorization) fits correctly in the blank as its usage in the sentence will indicate that the consent which was required before is not necessary anymore. 'Guide' as a noun means 'a person who advises others, especially in matters of behaviour or belief; advisor or mentor' and it will not make the sentence meaningful. 'Guidance or advice' would have fitted. 'Flinched' and 'Approve' are verbs, hence grammatically incorrect. Option A is the correct answer.

questions.question: Which word/phrase should come at (D)?
questions.explanation: A plural verb is needed in the blank to show the action performed by the plural subject 'reforms'. 'Assures' and 'ensures' are singular verbs, 'lies' is a singular verb or a plural noun and all of them are grammatically incorrect. Option D is the correct answer.

questions.question: Which word/phrase should come at (E)?
questions.explanation: A verb is required in the blank to show the action performed on the subject 'attempts'. And that verb will be in the past participle form as the sentence is in the present perfect tense and it is in the passive form. Only D fits. 'Assiduous' is an adjective, 'care' is a verb is in its root form and it is also a noun and 'prevent(verb)' is in its root form; all of them are grammatically incorrect. Undermined- lessen the effectiveness, power, or ability of, especially gradually or insidiously; option d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, choose the word opposite in meaning to the given word. Loquacious
questions.explanation: The word 'loquacious' means 'talkative'. 'Taciturn', which means 'reserved', is opposite in meaning to the given word. Hence, the answer is option c). Abstemious - non-indulgent; fastidious - very attentive to and concerned about accuracy and detail; mellifluous - sweet sounding.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, choose the one which best expresses the meaning of the given word. Tyrant
questions.explanation: The word 'abysmal' means extremely bad or appalling. The word 'pithy' means succinct. 'Prolix' is a person who is too verbose. The words 'tyrant' and 'dictator' are synonymous and refer to a cruel and oppressive ruler. The answer therefore is option d).

questions.question: In the following questions, choose the word opposite in meaning to the given word. Profound
questions.explanation: The word 'profound' refers to a great, intense or a deep emotion. 'Detest' is to loathe or hate someone. 'Turgid' language is tediously pompous or bombastic. 'Superficial' is opposite in meaning to 'profound' as it means something on the surface, mere pretence, not deep. Hence, the answer is option d).

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, choose the one which best expresses the meaning of the given word. Relinquish
questions.explanation: The meaning of 'Relinquish' is 'to give up power'. Hence the word that comes nearest to the meaning is 'Renounce', which means 'to give up material possession'. The answer is option a).

questions.question: In each of the questions below, an idiom is given, followed by four options. Select the option that gives the correct meaning of the idiom. play devil's advocate
options: To refuse to succumb to peer pressure
questions.explanation: 'To play devil's advocate' means 'to present a counter argument in order to promote a debate'. Clearly, the answer is option a).

questions.question: In each of the questions below, an idiom is given, followed by four options. Select the option that gives the correct meaning of the idiom. All brawn and no brains
options: Physically strong but not very intelligent
questions.explanation: 'All brawn and no brains' means 'physically strong but not intelligent'. Clearly, the answer is option a).

questions.question: In the question given below, a set of four sentences A, B, C, D is given, which when properly sequenced, form a coherent paragraph. Arrange the sentences in the correct order, and mark the correct order as your answer. A. While in the west, Allied Forces had fought their way through southern Italy as far as Rome. B. In June 1944 Germany's military position in World War II appeared hopeless. C. The disastrous situation was evident by the British Army's stand to drive the Nazis back through the east. D. The situation on the eastern front was catastrophic.
questions.explanation: There is a clear link between statement 'B' and 'D'; D gives the reason for the hopeless position of the German Army as mentioned in B; hence, the two will form a pair in same order. This is given only in option (b). 'C' (elaborates on the situation on the eastern front) and 'A' (tells us about the worsening situation in the west too, adding to the degrading position of Germany's military) follow logically. The correct order is BDCA and the answer is option b).

questions.question: Choose the option that fills the blank making the sentence, both semantically and syntactically, correct. The ... treatment meted out to women officers by not giving them too many duties, tough assignments and more of static jobs is also resented by male officers.
questions.explanation: Answer is 2. The idiomatic usage "kid glove treatment" means a treatment wherein someone is treated as very delicate or dainty. The rest of options are not in line with the context of the sentence. "Kids' stuff" means something very simple. "Kidult" means an adult who likes doing or buying things that are usually thought more suitable for children. "Kiddish" is a colloquial usage to call someone childish.

questions.question: Choose the option that fills the blank making the sentence, both semantically and syntactically, correct. The playwright is, of course, ... the false expertise of these apparently learned men by showing their knowledge to be no more than sophistry.
questions.explanation: Answer is 1 .The clue lies in the words "the playwright, showing their knowledge to be no more of sophistry." the manner of criticism that is used by a piece of writing to show the fault of an institution is a satire. Hence the other options are not closely related.

questions.question: A sentence/a part of the sentence is underlined. Four alternatives are given for the underlined part which will improve the sentence. Choose the correct alternative and click the button corresponding to it. In case no improvement is needed, click the button corresponding to "No improvement". There is no way you could have presumed the outcome of the match.
questions.explanation: The word 'preceded' means to come before something else, which makes no sense here. 'Preferred' means to like one thing better than another. 'Presumed' means assumed. A is the right answer, as the word 'predicted' means to foretell.

questions.question: A sentence/a part of the sentence is underlined. Four alternatives are given for the underlined part which will improve the sentence. Choose the correct alternative and click the button corresponding to it. In case no improvement is needed, click the button corresponding to "No improvement". They made a series of cruel jokes about Jerry, inferring that he was stupid.
questions.explanation: 'Infer' means to deduce, which does not make sense here. 'Deferring' means postponing, which does not fit here. 'Urging' means persuading, which does not have the correct meaning in this context. B is the right answer, as the word 'implying' means suggesting.

questions.question: Out of the four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words/sentences and mark it. Comfort in grief or sadness
questions.explanation: To proscribe is to forbid. A partisan is someone who is biased or committed to one side. Vapid means dull. Hence, the correct choice is solace.

questions.question: Out of the four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words/sentence. The ruler or a person who is tyrant
questions.explanation: Despot is a cruel and oppressive dictator Anarchist is an advocate of anarchism i.e. a political theory favouring the abolition of governments Righteous is characterized from accepted standards of morality or justice Saviour a person who rescues you from harm or danger

questions.question: Four words are given in which only one is correctly spelled. Find the correctly spelled word.
questions.explanation: Etiquette means a code of polite and mannerly conduct. There are three syllables in etiquette: et-i-quette. Example: We had a session on e-mail etiquette yesterday. The other words are correctly spelled as: Delightful, Alluring, Agreeable.

questions.question: Four words are given in which only one is correctly spelt. Find the correctly spelt word.
questions.explanation: Interpreted means understood. There are four syllables in the word interpreted: In-ter-pret-ed. Example: If everything works well, it means that the computer has interpreted our instructions well. The other words are correctly spelled as: Affection, Characteristic, Convenient.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, a sentence is given active/ passive voice. Change the statement from active to passive voice or vice versa, and choose the option that gives the correct statement. This sketch has been made by Monsieur Reynolds.
options: Monsieur Reynolds has made this sketch.
Monsieur Reynolds had made this sketch.
questions.explanation: The original sentence is in present perfect tense (has been made) and in passive voice. B is incorrect as 'had made' is past perfect tense. C is incorrect as 'made' is simple past tense. D is incorrect as 'makes' is simple present tense. A is the right answer as 'has made' is present perfect tense.

questions.question: In the following questions, a sentence has been given in Direct/ Indirect speech. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Direct/ Indirect speech. "My father is cooking dinner," said the girl.
options: The girl said that her father was cooking dinner.
The girl said that my father was cooking dinner.
The girl said that her father had been cooking dinner.
The girl said that her father cooks dinner.
questions.explanation: The first person possessive pronoun 'my' should change into the third person possessive 'her'. The present continuous tense 'is cooking' should change into the past continuous 'was cooking'. A is the right answer.

questions.question: In the question given below, a set of four sentences A, B, C, D is given, which when properly sequenced, form a coherent paragraph. Arrange the sentences in the correct order, and mark the correct order as your answer. A. The likelihood of an accident is determined by how carefully the motorist drives and how carefully the pedestrian crosses the street B. An accident involving a motorist and a pedestrian is such a case. C. Each must decide how much care to exercise without knowing how careful the other is D. The simplest strategic problem arises when two individuals interact with each other, and each must decide what to do without knowing what the other is doing.
questions.explanation: D begins the passage by stating about the simplest strategic problem arising when two individuals interact with each other, B is an example to such a problem, A tells us about the likelihood of an accident between a motorist and a pedestrian, C ends by stating how much care is to be exercised by each. The correct order is DBAC and the answer is option d).
